scenario.scale: IT service provider for municipal specialist procedures and distributed locations
scenario.context: Before a security round, it should become clear how protection needs analysis, risk classification, ISMS and technical measures relate to each other.
scenario.mission: Translate BSI standards, ISO 2700x, protection needs, risk classification and technical safeguards into a clear operational logic.

source.note: Management wants a clear separation between the standard framework, analysis and specific safeguards.

station.profile: BSI, ISMS, protection needs, risks and technical safeguards
constraints: Analysis, assessment and implementation must remain separate yet logically connected
Standards and handbook must not be confused with a single security tool
Technical measures should remain readable as concrete protective effects

customerRequest.subject: Prepare protection needs, ISMS and technical measures
customerRequest.body:
Hello,

please compile the terms BSI standards, ISO 2700x, ISMS, protection needs analysis and technical measures for the security round so that analysis, assessment and implementation remain clearly distinguishable.

Best regards
Information Security

contentBlocks.text: Several specialist procedures have been newly connected. The security round now wants to understand not only tools, but the logic of BSI, ISMS, protection needs, risk and technical measures.

questions.title: Recognize the BSI and ISO framework
questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the framework when BSI standards and ISO 2700x are mentioned?

options.text: BSI standards and ISO 2700x are recognized frameworks for structured information security; they do not replace concrete implementation in operations.
options.rationale: This is the most accurate classification.

options.text: BSI standards are just another name for firewall rules.
options.rationale: This confuses a framework with a single measure.

options.text: ISO 2700x applies only to paper files and not to information security.
Once a standard is named, nothing else needs to be checked in operations.
options.rationale: A standard never replaces operational implementation.

feedback.correct: The standard framework was classified correctly.
feedback.wrong: Think framework, not a single tool.

questions.title: Derive protection needs and requirements
questions.prompt: Which statements relate to protection needs analysis, risk classification and deriving security requirements? Multiple statements can be correct.

options.text: Protection needs of information and systems are determined first.
options.rationale: Without protection needs there is no solid prioritization.

options.text: Risks are classified by significance and urgency.
options.rationale: This helps control the order of measures.

options.text: Security requirements are derived from protection needs.
options.rationale: That is exactly what connects analysis and implementation.

options.text: Measures should then be coordinated, implemented and evaluated.
options.rationale: Implementation is part of reliable security work.

options.text: A firewall alone replaces protection needs analysis and risk classification.
options.rationale: A single tool does not replace the analysis process.

options.text: It only makes sense to determine protection needs after an incident.
options.rationale: Then the steering comes too late.

feedback.correct: The analysis and implementation logic was correctly recognized.
feedback.wrong: First protection needs, then risks, then requirements and measures.

questions.title: Structure a security analysis
questions.prompt: How does a clean analysis of security requirements typically proceed?

feedback.correct: The sequence of the security analysis is correct.
feedback.wrong: First identify, then assess, derive, implement and review.

questions.title: Assign BSI and IT security terms
questions.prompt: Which description best fits each term?

pairs.right: Methodological framework of the BSI for baseline protection and security management.
Collection of modules and measures for baseline protection.
Coordinates security tasks and serves as the point of contact.
Grant access only to authorized persons.
Make data unreadable without a key.
Verify the identity of a person or system.

options: Methodological framework of the BSI for baseline protection and security management.
Collection of modules and measures for baseline protection.
Coordinates security tasks and serves as the point of contact.
Grant access only to authorized persons.
Make data unreadable without a key.
Verify the identity of a person or system.

feedback.correct: The security terms were assigned consistently.
feedback.wrong: Check the difference between framework, role, control and technical effect.

questions.title: Count the protection goals
questions.prompt: How many classic protection goals are considered here?

feedback.wrong: The classic classification includes confidentiality, integrity and availability.
